header_summary:
Antoine's discussion on enhancing Bitcoin's coinbase transaction uniqueness post-BIP34, including the proposed BIP54, highlights ongoing efforts to refine the protocol's security and efficiency. He elucidates on historical issues addressed by BIP30 and suggests BIP54 as a more effective solution for transaction uniqueness, potentially allowing for the retirement of BIP30's checks. This exploration underscores the Bitcoin development community's commitment to solving complex challenges within the network. [Read more](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/e758af9b-72fc-4fdd-8e07-e1126635780an@googlegroups.com/T/#me12871832aef23d2776f0fbdcab2a261bba25008).

A novel quantum-secure vault scheme utilizing OP_CHECKTEMPLATEVERIFY and OP_TXHASH is discussed, showcasing a multi-phase secret-reveal strategy resistant to quantum attacks. This approach highlights the innovative use of existing Bitcoin protocol features to enhance security against quantum computing threats, emphasizing the scheme's potential for safeguarding assets through predetermined transaction paths and secure secret-reveal mechanisms. [Further details](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/CAJowKgJnvHr+WV7+_52prP_QqiL=+zWe+hBMLmsCbO2san2NbQ@mail.gmail.com/T/#u#ma4372d256de91beec5a6039e9611d433380e3231).

The complexity of implementing Falcon, a NIST Post-Quantum Cryptography scheme, for bitcoin-like applications is highlighted, with discussions on its signature overhead and the challenges of software emulation for floating point arithmetic. The dialogue sheds light on the critical need for adaptable, secure cryptographic solutions in anticipation of Cryptographically Relevant Quantum Computing developments, exploring alternative architectures and the potential of stateful post-quantum signatures for enhancing security and privacy in blockchain technologies. [Learn more](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/shrincs-324-byte-stateful-post-quantum-signatures-with-static-backups/2158/14).

Finally, the conversation on using block height versus physical time for scheduling blockchain transactions reveals the unpredictability of block generation rates, advocating for block height as a more reliable reference for transaction timing. This discussion contributes to the broader dialogue on optimizing blockchain transaction scheduling, emphasizing the importance of accuracy and predictability in transaction execution within the blockchain framework. [Read further](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/scheduled-delayed-transaction-broadcast/2148/9).

recent_posts.title: Addressing remaining points on BIP 54
recent_posts.summary:
- Antoine discusses enhancing Bitcoin's coinbase transaction uniqueness, focusing on BIP34 and proposed BIP54.
- He points out issues with pre-BIP34 coinbase transactions and suggests BIP54 for better uniqueness.
- Antoine recommends using nLocktime for coinbase uniqueness, dismissing concerns over cryptoeconomic attacks.

recent_posts.title: SImple quantum security, at the expense of slower tx time
recent_posts.summary:
- A quantum-secure vault creation method uses secret-reveal and OP_CHECKTEMPLATEVERIFY, resisting quantum threats.
- The strategy includes a multi-phase process with secure value transfer into an Anchor envelope through TXHASH.
- It features quantum signature safety, reorg resistance, and an escape hatch, enhancing security against quantum attacks.

recent_posts.title: Falcon Post-Quantum Signature Scheme Proposal
recent_posts.summary:
- Concerns about implementing Falcon for Bitcoin due to its complexity and error risks were discussed.
- There's a debate on Falcon's suitability for Bitcoin, with suggestions for alternative schemes.
- XMSS signatures were recommended for their efficiency and the added security of discouraging address reuse.

recent_posts.title: BLISK: Boolean circuit Logic Integrated into the Single Key
recent_posts.summary:
- The BLISK approach enhances cryptographic security by making secret materials derivable, reducing storage complexity.
- It prevents cheating in OR-gate constructions, enabling efficient, non-interactive verification processes.
- Utilizes zero-knowledge proofs for secure key rotation, allowing public verification without widespread distribution.

recent_posts.title: OP_CAT and Bitcoin’s Path to Quantum Resistance
recent_posts.summary:
- This summary outlines a cybersecurity threat known as "short-range" attack.
- Short-range attacks require the attacker to be physically close to the target's devices.
- Recognizing these attacks is vital for improving cybersecurity defenses.

recent_posts.title: Scheduled (Delayed) Transaction Broadcast
recent_posts.summary:
- The analysis compares block height and physical time in scheduling blockchain transactions.
- It finds significant variance in block production, affecting transaction timing accuracy.
- The recommendation favors block height for its predictability in transaction scheduling.

active_posts.title: Addressing remaining points on BIP 54
active_posts.summary:
- Luke Dashjr criticizes BIP 54 for compromising ASIC optimization by altering nLockTime.
- Jeremy Rubin proposes a sparse Merkle tree over limiting transaction sizes to avoid smart contract issues.
- The consensus favors pragmatic solutions, avoiding drastic changes to maintain the ecosystem's stability.

active_posts.title: Falcon Post-Quantum Signature Scheme Proposal
active_posts.summary:
- A soft fork integrates the Falcon post-quantum signature scheme into Bitcoin Core.
- Falcon offers smaller key sizes and faster verification than current Bitcoin cryptography.
- The project aims to benchmark Falcon's performance for future Bitcoin security enhancements.

active_posts.title: SImple quantum security, at the expense of slower tx time
active_posts.summary:
- A quantum-secure vault leverages OP_CTV and SHA256 for resistance to quantum attacks.
- The approach separates execution triggers from value destinations to secure assets.
- It features quantum signature safety, reorg resistance, and an escape route for added security.

active_posts.title: SHRINCS: 324-byte stateful post-quantum signatures with static backups
active_posts.summary:
- SHRINCS merges stateful hash-based and stateless signatures for efficient, robust operation.
- It uses unbalanced XMSS trees and SPHINCS+ variants for dual-mode functionality, enhancing security.
- The scheme, particularly useful in Bitcoin wallets, ensures security even when state management fails.

active_posts.title: Disposing of "dust attack" UTXOs
active_posts.summary:
- Dust attack UTXOs pose a privacy risk by linking wallet transactions.
- Mitigation strategies include locking dust UTXOs, though this has its own future risks.
- A proposed solution suggests spending dust on fees, but raises new challenges.

active_posts.title: Major BIP 360 Update
active_posts.summary:
- Ethan Heilman and two others have completely rewritten BIP 360 for clarity.
- The update introduces "Pay-to-Tapscript-Hash" to improve Bitcoin's quantum resistance.
- It also adds test vectors and invites community review to enhance security.

today_in_history_posts.title: Purely off-chain coin colouring
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Casey Rodarmor introduced ordinals for NFTs on bitcoin, tracking satoshis' lifetimes.
- Inscriptions add data to ordinals, potentially moving entirely off-chain for efficiency.
- The proposal highlights challenges and alternatives, like Liquid's model for semi-fungible tokens.

today_in_history_posts.title: Local Reputation to Mitigate Slow Jamming
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- The development team proposes a reputation setting to prevent Lightning Network jamming.
- Reputation and upfront fees determine transaction forwarding to combat "slow jamming." - Parameters for reputation involve time, potential damage, and revenue generated from fees.

today_in_history_posts.title: 0conf LN channels and v3 anchors
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- The LN spec call identified a v3 transaction issue with 0conf channel support.
- Unconfirmed ancestors in v3 transactions create non-broadcastable packages, hindering channel closures.
- Adjusting v3 rules could allow transactions from confirmed inputs, enabling structured payment chains.
